key_quote: C++ atomics achieves only 9% exact-match (1/11) while aggregate 81.2% hides per-category failure — a developer using std::atomic experiences 9% accuracy
rationale: The tool addresses a genuine, urgent developer pain point (x86-to-ARM migration) with strong formal artifacts (55 UNSAT certificates). But C++ atomics accuracy is catastrophic (9%), 82% of differential tests are trivial, fence costs are ungrounded analytical weights, Theorem 3's biconditional is incorrect, GPU models lack SMT validation, and there's zero build system integration. If implementation were perfected, no reviewer would say 'not novel'. This is Category B: novel and timely concept, incomplete and inflated execution.
